title: dysphania dissecta
intro: Dysphania dissecta, also known as Epazote or Jew's weed, is a flowering plant in the amaranth family, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural insect repellent and anthelmintic, helping to expel intestinal worms. The health benefits of Dysphania dissecta include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. Dysphania dissecta can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking to flavor dishes, particularly beans and legumes, due to its strong, pungent aroma.
